contextBlurb: New York's 2025-2026 legislative session includes several housing bills addressing zoning reform, tenant protections, and affordable housing financing. Key measures include S401 (Tenant Opportunity to Purchase Act), A6283A/S3492A (Sustainable Affordable Housing and Sprawl Prevention Act), and A3647B (Faith-Based Affordable Housing Act). The FY 2026 Enacted Budget also secured over $1.5 billion in new state housing funding, including the first $50 million for the Housing Access Voucher Program.

legislation.title: State Education, Labor, Housing and Family Assistance Budget Bill FY 2025-2026
legislation.summary: Enacts major components of legislation necessary to implement the state housing and family assistance budget for 2025-2026, including over $1.5 billion in new housing funding.

legislation.title: Tenant Opportunity to Purchase Act
legislation.summary: Establishes the Tenant Opportunity to Purchase Act to prevent displacement of middle and lower-income tenants in New York and preserve affordable housing by giving tenants the right to purchase their buildings.

legislation.title: Sustainable Affordable Housing and Sprawl Prevention Act
legislation.summary: Exempts or limits environmental review under SEQR for the construction of certain new affordable housing developments to streamline housing production and reduce sprawl.

legislation.title: Sustainable Affordable Housing and Sprawl Prevention Act
legislation.summary: Senate companion bill that exempts or limits environmental review under SEQR for the construction of certain new affordable housing to accelerate development approvals.

legislation.title: Faith-Based Affordable Housing Act
legislation.summary: Amends the general municipal law to enact the Faith-Based Affordable Housing Act, enabling residential development on religious land to expand housing supply.

legislation.title: Employer-Assisted Housing Match Grant Program
legislation.summary: Creates an employer-assisted housing match grant program under which certain nonprofit employees may be eligible for a state match for certain housing-related contributions.
